publicationStmt.publisher: Schenker Documents Online in association with the Centre for Computing in the Humanities

sourceDesc.p: No source: created in electronic format.

encodingDesc.p: Encoding according to the CCH TEI Guidelines

div.p: Book and sheet music shop in Kiel.

Walter G. Mühlau was a retailer of books and sheet music, and small-scale publisher, situated at Brunswiker Straße 29a, Kiel. In 2002 it celebrated having been the Kiel University book shop for 100 years.

div.head: Walter G. Mühlau and the Schenker circle
div.p: Mühlau was the local bookshop of Schenker’s pupil Reinhard Oppel, who taught at the Kiel Conservatory between 1911 and 1924 and at Kiel University from 1924 to 1931, and therefore no doubt of his pupils and colleagues in music.

div.head: Correspondence
div.p: There is no known correspondence between Mühlau and Heinrich Schenker, nor is Mühlau mentioned in Schenker’s diary. There is one surviving postcard from Mühlau to Reinhard Oppel from 1929 (OJ 71/25, [1]) concerning Schenker’s unpublished elucidatory edition of Beethoven’s Piano Sonata Op. 106, and there are three receipts from Mühlau to the Kiel Musicological Seminar, Oppel, and a Mr. Timm (OC 52/641, 642, and 643) for payment for issues of Der Tonwille in January and March 1925. (These receipts were the result of Schenker’s dispute with Universal Edition over inadequate sales of Der Tonwille in 1924 and 1925, and his calling upon his pupils to whip up subscriptions to the series and to check on numbers, most of the receipts coming from Albert J. Gutmann or Universal Edition itself.)
